title: Radish Salad Recipe
summary: This radish salad is a vibrant, zesty dish that perfectly awakens your taste buds with its refreshing and gingery flavour. Ideal as a side or a light main, this radish salad recipe combines crisp radishes with a punchy vinaigrette, making it a delightful addition to your green salads repertoire. It pairs wonderfully with rice, offering a contrasting texture and flavour. For a complete meal, enjoy it alongside a sriracha fried egg or a protein-rich salmon fillet. This radish salad is not only a refreshing choice but also complements a variety of dishes, including those on a Mediterranean diet or served with pita bread. Enhance the flavour profile with a touch of white wine vinegar or fresh dill for an extra layer of freshness. Perfect for a picnic or as a quick, nutritious lunch, this radish salad will quickly become a favourite in your salad rotation.

method.description: Toast the coriander seeds in a small dry frying pan over a high heat for 1-2 mins until smelling amazing, then tip into a pestle and mortar or spice grinder and leave to cool. Once cool, roughly grind.

Peel the ginger then finely grate half of it into a large bowl. Add the sesame oil, a splash of olive oil and the zest and juice of the lime. Whisk together with a fork to make a dressing, adding sugar, salt and pepper to taste, as well as more lime juice if you like a sour kick.
Cut the remaining ginger into as thin matchsticks as you can, then finely slice the radishes and cucumber into rounds.

Add the radishes, cucumber and ginger matchsticks to the dressing bowl, along with the ground toasted coriander. Pick in most of the mint and coriander leaves. Roughly chop the cashews, add most of them to the bowl, then toss the salad together in the dressing so that everything gets nicely combined.
Pile the salad onto a platter. Top with the remaining cashews, mint and coriander then sprinkle over a few chilli flakes to serve, if you like.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
